scenarios.rationale: S2 found that a running back's usage history stops predicting after a team change (corrected gap -0.279, holdout -0.302) while a receiver's survives (holdout -0.006). Any engine change that discounts moved players must keep that asymmetry: discounting receivers as hard as backs would be acting on an effect the data does not support. This re-runs the real measurement from cached parquet and asserts the RB disruption is both material and strictly larger than the WR one. Degrades to a non-crashing miss when the context table is absent.
